summary: Executed S1-04 validation across source regressions, lint/build, and production UI flow with professional credentials. All targeted regressions passed and quick-book creation succeeded on production, but get_available_slots RPC was not observable in production quick-book network traces.

integration_issues.issue: On https://cut-sync.vercel.app, opening quick-book modal and changing service/date did not emit /rest/v1/rpc/get_available_slots in captured requests/resources, even though create_appointment succeeded.
Repeated 403 responses observed for get_appointment_participant_names during dashboard updates. Flow remained usable but requires backend policy/authorization review.

issues: Desktop prev/next arrows still move selected date by 7 days per click (legacy behavior), which remains outside S1-04 scope but conflicts with the broader PRD expectation for daily navigation.

action_items: Investigate production quick-book availability path to ensure get_available_slots is actually called and not served from stale/static data path.
Review Supabase/RPC authorization for get_appointment_participant_names to eliminate repeated 403s on professional dashboard refresh.
After fix/redeploy, retest S1-04 header/timeline openings and availability state transitions on production with network tracing enabled.
critical_code_review_comments: Source implementation for S1-04 is coherent and testable (explicit quickBookSource, submit lock, known RPC error mapping, centralized availability hook usage).
Production behavior is partially inconsistent with source intent because availability RPC traces were not visible while quick-book remained operational.

success_rate.frontend: 80% (core quick-book creation validated; availability RPC trace mismatch remains)

seed_data_creation: Created one production quick booking via professional account during validation (client_name: 'Teste S1-04 Playwright').
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/memory/test_credentials.md professional account. Reproduce on https://cut-sync.vercel.app > /login > /professional > Encaixe rápido. Capture /rest/v1/rpc/* requests and confirm get_available_slots appears when selecting/changing service/date in quick-book modal.
rca of the issue: RCA hypothesis: deployed production bundle/path may still be using an older or indirect availability mechanism for professional quick-book UI rendering, because explicit get_available_slots traffic was not observed despite source tests proving hook integration and create_appointment path working. Reproduction: login as professional, open quick-book, change service/date, inspect network for /rpc/get_available_slots; expected call absent in this run.
test_credentials: Read from /app/memory/test_credentials.md and used professional account brusantos7178@outlook.com / bruno1324.
